name: Cache-aside with TTL
description: Application reads cache first and fills it from database on miss
when_to_use: Read-heavy data product catalogs profiles permissions and dashboards
trade_offs: Stale reads are possible until TTL or invalidation
implementation_notes: Use short TTLs explicit invalidation on writes and dogpile protection
keywords: redis cache aside ttl read heavy

name: Write-through cache
description: Write data to cache and durable store in the same path
when_to_use: Data that must be immediately available after write
trade_offs: Write latency and failure handling become more complex
implementation_notes: Use only where cache consistency matters more than write simplicity

name: Event-driven invalidation
description: Publish domain events to remove or refresh affected cache entries
when_to_use: Mutable data shared by multiple services or workers
trade_offs: Requires reliable event delivery and idempotent handlers
implementation_notes: Use versioned keys or outbox events for critical invalidation
keywords: cache invalidation event outbox redis pubsub

name: Redis session store
description: Centralized low-latency session or token metadata store
when_to_use: Horizontal web servers and session revocation needs
trade_offs: Redis outage can affect login/session behavior
implementation_notes: Set TTL align with session policy and use secure cookie identifiers

name: Distributed lock with fencing token
description: Coordinate rare cross-process critical sections
when_to_use: Single resource jobs scheduled tasks or deduplicated workers
trade_offs: Locks are easy to misuse and can cause data corruption
implementation_notes: Prefer database constraints first; if needed include expiry and fencing token

name: Request coalescing
description: Collapse concurrent cache misses into one database or API call
when_to_use: Hot keys expensive external API calls and thundering herd risk
trade_offs: Adds in-flight request bookkeeping
implementation_notes: Use singleflight or promise memoization with timeout and fallback

name: Versioned cache keys
description: Include schema or entity version in cache keys
when_to_use: Changing cached payload shape or invalidating broad sets
trade_offs: Old keys remain until TTL expires
implementation_notes: Use predictable prefixes and bump version on incompatible payload change

name: Negative caching
description: Cache known misses for a short TTL
when_to_use: Hot lookup misses usernames slugs permissions and external resources
trade_offs: Can hide newly created data until TTL expires
implementation_notes: Use very short TTL and invalidate on create
keywords: negative cache miss not found ttl

name: HTTP edge caching
description: Cache public or semi-public HTTP responses near users
when_to_use: Static catalog pages docs pricing and public content
trade_offs: Personalized data must not leak through shared cache
implementation_notes: Use cache-control vary etag and surrogate keys

name: Stale-while-revalidate
description: Serve stale content while refreshing in background
when_to_use: Read-heavy data where slight staleness is acceptable
trade_offs: Requires clear freshness semantics
implementation_notes: Use stale window and background refresh with failure fallback
keywords: stale while revalidate swr cache refresh

name: Token bucket rate cache
description: Use Redis counters for distributed rate limiting
when_to_use: Public APIs auth endpoints and tenant quotas
trade_offs: Clock and key design affect fairness
implementation_notes: Use atomic increments or Lua scripts with expiry
keywords: redis rate limit token bucket quota

name: Cache PII minimization
description: Avoid storing sensitive personal data in shared caches
when_to_use: Healthcare finance auth and regulated domains
trade_offs: May reduce cache hit rate
implementation_notes: Cache IDs or derived non-sensitive fields and encrypt if unavoidable

name: Write-behind cache
description: Buffer writes in cache and persist asynchronously
when_to_use: High-throughput counters analytics and non-critical telemetry
trade_offs: Risk of data loss if cache fails before flush
implementation_notes: Use only for non-critical data with durable fallback

name: Read-through cache
description: Cache layer loads data automatically on miss
when_to_use: Services using cache abstraction libraries
trade_offs: Can hide database coupling in cache layer
implementation_notes: Keep loader observable and apply TTL per data type

name: Lease based refresh
description: One process refreshes an expiring hot key
when_to_use: Hot cache keys and expensive recomputation
trade_offs: Lease bugs can block refresh
implementation_notes: Use short lease TTL and serve stale value during refresh

name: Cache stampede lock
description: Protect database from many concurrent misses
when_to_use: Popular keys after expiry and traffic spikes
trade_offs: Lock service can become bottleneck
implementation_notes: Use jittered TTL request coalescing or lock with timeout

name: Tenant-scoped cache key
description: Include tenant or workspace identifier in every key
when_to_use: Multi-tenant SaaS shared Redis and permission caches
trade_offs: Long keys and invalidation complexity
implementation_notes: Use prefix tenant:<id>:domain:<key> and never share mutable keys

name: Permission cache invalidation
description: Invalidate authz cache on role or policy changes
when_to_use: RBAC ABAC and enterprise permissions
trade_offs: Frequent invalidation can lower hit rate
implementation_notes: Use short TTL plus event invalidation for role updates

name: Object cache for rendered documents
description: Cache expensive generated artifacts
when_to_use: PDF reports invoices thumbnails and exports
trade_offs: Invalidation and storage lifecycle need policy
implementation_notes: Store by content hash or source version and expiry

name: Local in-process cache
description: Cache tiny stable data inside one process
when_to_use: Feature flags config reference data and compiled schemas
trade_offs: Not shared across instances and can go stale
implementation_notes: Use bounded size TTL and explicit reload path

name: Redis pubsub invalidation
description: Broadcast cache invalidation to app instances
when_to_use: Multi-instance apps with local caches
trade_offs: Pubsub messages can be missed
implementation_notes: Combine with versioned keys or TTL as safety net

name: Cache warming job
description: Preload critical cache entries before traffic
when_to_use: High-traffic launches dashboards and known hot resources
trade_offs: Can waste work for unused keys
implementation_notes: Warm only proven hot keys and measure hit rate

name: Cache hit ratio metric
description: Measure cache effectiveness by key group
when_to_use: Any production cache
trade_offs: Cannot optimize what is not measured
implementation_notes: Track hit miss latency evictions and stale serves

name: Eviction policy selection
description: Choose cache eviction based on access pattern
when_to_use: Redis shared caches and memory constrained systems
trade_offs: Wrong policy can evict critical keys
implementation_notes: Use allkeys-lru volatile-ttl or separate Redis by workload

name: Cache bypass control
description: Allow operators to bypass cache for diagnosis
when_to_use: Debugging stale data incidents and emergency mitigation
trade_offs: Can overload origin if abused
implementation_notes: Gate bypass to admin or internal requests and log usage
